enonce.texte: Transform the direct commands into reported speech using 'tell' or 'ask'.

questions.texte: "Sit down!" the teacher said to the students.
questions.reponse: The teacher told the students to sit down.

questions.texte: "Please help me," she said to her friend.
questions.reponse: She asked her friend to help her.

questions.texte: "Don't be late!" my father said to me.
questions.reponse: My father told me not to be late.

questions.texte: "Could you open the window?" he said to her.
questions.reponse: He asked her to open the window.

questions.texte: "Don't touch the painting!" the guard said.
questions.reponse: The guard told me not to touch the painting.

sousQuestions.texte: What is the correct reported speech?
sousQuestions.reponse: The guard told me not to touch the painting.

questions.texte: "Please turn down the music," my sister said.
questions.reponse: My sister asked me to turn down the music.

sousQuestions.texte: What is the correct reported speech?
sousQuestions.reponse: My sister asked me to turn down the music.

questions.texte: "Wait for me!" he said to his friend.
questions.reponse: He told his friend to wait for him.

sousQuestions.texte: What is the correct reported speech?
sousQuestions.reponse: He told his friend to wait for him.

enonce.texte:
Read the dialogue and rewrite the requests in reported speech.

At the reception desk:

  • Guest: "Could you give me my room key?"
  • Receptionist: "Please fill in this form first."
  • Guest: "Can you call a taxi for me?"
  • Receptionist: "Sure. Don't worry about it."
  • Guest: "Please send someone to clean my room."
  • Receptionist: "I'll send someone right away."
images: A hotel reception desk with a guest talking to a receptionist

questions.texte: Rewrite the guest's requests in reported speech.
questions.reponse: The guest asked the receptionist to give him/her the room key. The guest also asked the receptionist to call a taxi. Then the guest asked the receptionist to send someone to clean his/her room.

sousQuestions.texte: Rewrite the receptionist's responses as reported speech.
sousQuestions.reponse: The receptionist asked the guest to fill in the form first. The receptionist told the guest not to worry about it. The receptionist said he/she would send someone right away.

enonce.texte:
Read the situation and write the reported commands and requests.

A teacher is giving instructions to her students before an exam. She says:

  • "Turn off your phones."
  • "Don't talk during the exam."
  • "Please write your name at the top of the paper."
  • "Don't use correction fluid."
  • "Could you raise your hand if you have a question?"

questions.texte: Rewrite the teacher's instructions in reported speech.
questions.reponse: The teacher told the students to turn off their phones. She told them not to talk during the exam. She asked them to write their name at the top of the paper. She told them not to use correction fluid. She asked them to raise their hand if they had a question.

enonce.texte: Rewrite the following orders in reported speech using 'told' or 'ordered'.

questions.texte: "Stop the car!" the police officer shouted.
questions.reponse: The police officer ordered me to stop the car.

questions.texte: "Do your homework now!" my mother said.
questions.reponse: My mother told me to do my homework then.

questions.texte: "Don't forget to buy milk," my aunt said.
questions.reponse: My aunt told me not to forget to buy milk.

enonce.texte: Change these polite requests into reported speech using 'ask' or 'request'.

questions.texte: "Could you lend me your pen?" she said to him.
questions.reponse: She asked him to lend her his pen.

questions.texte: "Would you mind closing the door?" he said.
questions.reponse: He asked me to close the door.

questions.texte: "Can you help me with this exercise?" the student said to the teacher.
questions.reponse: The student asked the teacher to help him/her with the exercise.

enonce.texte: Create a dialogue where one person gives commands and requests, and another person responds. Then, write the reported speech version of the dialogue.

questions.texte: Write a dialogue (5-6 exchanges) and its reported version.
questions.reponse:
Example dialogue:
A: Please bring me the book.
B: Here it is.
A: Could you open the window?
B: Sure.
A: Don't forget to close the door when you leave.
B: Okay, I won't.

Reported version:
A asked B to bring him/her the book. B did. A asked B to open the window. B said he/she would. A told B not to forget to close the door when he/she left. B said he/she wouldn't.

enonce.texte: Match the direct speech with the correct reported speech.

sousQuestions.reponse: She told me to sit there.
The teacher told us not to run.
He asked me to come in.
My father told me not to be late.
